<!-- Autogenerated guidance for Copilot-style AI agents working on this repo -->
# Copilot Instructions — com.zain.uber

Purpose: quickly orient an AI coding assistant to the project's structure, patterns, and developer workflows so edits are safe and productive.

- Quick facts:
  - **Language:** Java 17
  - **Framework:** Spring Boot 4.x (parent in `pom.xml`)
  - **Builds:** Maven primary (`pom.xml`) — wrappers present (`mvnw.cmd`, `gradlew.bat`) but prefer Maven for canonical commands
  - **Runtime DB:** H2 (in-memory at runtime; dependency in `pom.xml`)

- High-level architecture:
  - Layered web app: HTTP controllers -> service layer -> JPA repositories -> entity model.
  - Controller example: [src/main/java/com/zain/uber/controller/RideController.java](src/main/java/com/zain/uber/controller/RideController.java#L1-L200)
  - Service example: [src/main/java/com/zain/uber/service/RideService.java](src/main/java/com/zain/uber/service/RideService.java#L1-L200)
  - Repository example: [src/main/java/com/zain/uber/repository/RideRepo.java](src/main/java/com/zain/uber/repository/RideRepo.java#L1-L200)
  - DTO: [src/main/java/com/zain/uber/dto/RiderInfo.java](src/main/java/com/zain/uber/dto/RiderInfo.java#L1-L200)
  - Entity: [src/main/java/com/zain/uber/entity/Rider.java](src/main/java/com/zain/uber/entity/Rider.java#L1-L200)

- Typical data flow (concrete):
  - HTTP POST to `/ride/save-ride` handled by `RideController.saveRide()` which delegates to `RideService.saveRide()` and persists via `RideRepo.save(...)`.

- Project-specific observations and gotchas (use these as guardrails):
  - `RideService` uses field injection with `@Autowired` on the repo: follow that pattern when adding quick fixes (but prefer constructor injection for larger refactors and ask the team first).
  - `RideController` in the current source has a private `RideService rideService` field but no constructor/@Autowired — watch for uninitialized fields. If you modify controller wiring, ensure dependency injection is correctly added (constructor or `@Autowired`).
  - DTO vs Entity: `RiderInfo` (DTO) and `Rider` (entity) coexist but there is no mapping utility. When changing persistence code, add explicit mapping (constructor or small mapper method) rather than assuming JPA will accept a DTO.

- Build / run / test (Windows examples):
  - Run app (Maven wrapper): `mvnw.cmd spring-boot:run`
  - Package: `mvnw.cmd -DskipTests package` (or `mvnw.cmd package` to run tests)
  - Run tests: `mvnw.cmd test`
  - Gradle alternative (if needed): `gradlew.bat bootRun`

- Files to check when implementing features or debugging:
  - App entry: [src/main/java/com/zain/uber/UberApplication.java](src/main/java/com/zain/uber/UberApplication.java#L1-L80)
  - App config: [src/main/resources/application.yaml](src/main/resources/application.yaml#L1-L40)
  - Build: [pom.xml](pom.xml#L1-L120)

- Conventions to follow in pull requests:
  - Keep changes layered (controller/service/repo) and small — modify DTO<->Entity mapping explicitly.
  - Prefer minimal, non-invasive fixes when wiring or missing annotations are involved; flag larger refactors in PR descriptions.
  - Use existing dependencies (H2, Spring Boot starter) rather than adding new infra without team review.

- When unsure, inspect these examples first (they show the canonical patterns used today):
  - `RideController.saveRide()` -> `RideService.saveRide()` -> `RideRepo.save()`
  - DTO: `RiderInfo` and entity: `Rider` for field names and expected shape

If anything here is unclear or you'd like more/less detail (examples, mapping snippets, or preferred DI style), tell me what to expand and I will iterate.
